output.summary: Fix two bugs: (1) Per-tab color not applied to wrapper background on desktop — selectTab() in Tabs.js always applies bg-accent-lighter instead of reading data-tab-color and adding the color class so the CSS custom-variant redefines --accent-color. (2) Mobile border disappears on open — the details element uses open:bg-accent which works, but open:border-accent alone does not visually persist the border because the border utility (border border-charcoal) competes with the open state color; needs explicit open:border to maintain border width plus open:border-accent for the color change.

fileChanges.description: Update selectTab() to read data-tab-color from the active wrapper and apply the color class instead of (or in addition to) bg-accent-lighter. When data-tab-color is empty, fall back to bg-accent-lighter. Also clean up color classes from previously active wrappers.
fileChanges.details:
In the selectTab() function, lines 88-97:

1. Define a list of valid color classes: ['neon-green', 'blue', 'green', 'yellow', 'orange', 'purple'].

2. In the wrappers.forEach loop, for the ACTIVE wrapper (i === index):
   a. Read the color: const tabColor = wrapper.dataset.tabColor || '';
   b. Remove all color classes from the wrapper: validColors.forEach(c => wrapper.classList.remove(c));
   c. If tabColor is non-empty AND is in the valid list, add the tabColor class to wrapper AND add bg-accent-lighter (which will resolve to the recolored accent via the CSS custom-variant).
   d. If tabColor is empty, just add bg-accent-lighter (existing behavior, uses page accent).
   e. Keep existing md:hidden removal and md:flex addition.

3. For INACTIVE wrappers:
   a. Remove all color classes: validColors.forEach(c => wrapper.classList.remove(c));
   b. Remove bg-accent-lighter (existing behavior).
   c. Keep existing md:hidden addition and md:flex removal.

4. In setMobileMode(), also clean up color classes from all wrappers so they don't leak into mobile view:
   wrappers.forEach(w => { validColors.forEach(c => w.classList.remove(c)); w.classList.remove('bg-accent-lighter'); });

The key insight: The CSS custom-variant system (e.g., .blue { --accent-color: var(--color-blue); --accent-color-lighter: var(--color-blue-lighter); }) means adding the color class like 'blue' to the wrapper will redefine --accent-color-lighter within that scope, so bg-accent-lighter will automatically resolve to the correct lighter shade of that color. No new CSS is needed.

fileChanges.description: Fix mobile border persistence on open state by ensuring the border width is explicitly maintained when open.
fileChanges.details:
On line 21 (the details element class_name array), the current classes include:
  'open:bg-accent open:border-accent' => true,

The issue: 'border border-charcoal' sets both border-width (1px) and border-color (charcoal). When the details is opened, 'open:border-accent' only changes the border-color to the accent color, which should work. However, if there is a CSS specificity or cascade issue, the border may not visually appear.

Investigation suggests the actual problem may be that md:border-0 is being applied on mobile due to a CSS specificity issue, OR the open:border-accent is working but the visual change is too subtle. Let's verify the class list.

Looking at the current classes:
- 'border border-charcoal' - sets 1px border with charcoal color
- 'open:bg-accent open:border-accent' - on open, changes bg and border color
- 'md:border-0' - removes border on desktop

The md: prefix should only apply at >= 60rem (960px). On mobile (< 960px), md:border-0 should NOT apply. So the border should persist.

Possible root cause: In Tailwind v4, the 'border' utility provides border-width but 'open:border-accent' only sets border-color. If there is a specificity battle where 'md:border-0' is somehow winning even on mobile, or if the border-color change to accent (which may be similar to the background) makes it look like the border disappeared.

The fix: Add 'open:border' explicitly to ensure border-width is re-asserted on open, alongside 'open:border-accent' for the color. Change:
  'open:bg-accent open:border-accent' => true,
To:
  'open:bg-accent open:border open:border-accent' => true,

This guarantees the 1px border is explicitly set when open, preventing any cascade issue from removing it.

fileChanges.description: Mirror the PHP border fix in the editor component for consistency.
fileChanges.details:
In the details element className cn() object (around line 108), change:
  'open:bg-accent open:border-accent': true,
To:
  'open:bg-accent open:border open:border-accent': true,

This mirrors the PHP fix so the editor preview matches the frontend.

designDecisions.decision: Use CSS custom-variant color classes on wrapper to redefine --accent-color-lighter, then bg-accent-lighter resolves automatically to the per-tab color
designDecisions.evidence: colors.css defines .blue, .orange, .purple etc. as custom-variants that redefine --accent-color and --accent-color-lighter. The tab buttons already use this pattern (tabColor class on button element). Applying the same class to the wrapper gives bg-accent-lighter the correct color scope.

designDecisions.decision: Add explicit open:border to maintain border-width on mobile open state
designDecisions.evidence: User reports border disappearing on mobile open. Adding open:border re-asserts 1px border-width alongside open:border-accent color change, preventing any cascade/specificity loss.

designDecisions.decision: Clean up color classes from wrappers when switching tabs and when entering mobile mode to prevent color leaking
designDecisions.evidence: The selectTab function already manages bg-accent-lighter add/remove. Color classes need the same lifecycle management to avoid stale color scopes on inactive wrappers or in mobile mode.
